evals.name: contains-18-substring
evals.prompt: add a perk called 'חי' described 'מספר לוחית מכיל 18' for when the plate number contains 18
evals.expected_output: One new PLATE_PERKS entry: name 'חי', desc 'מספר לוחית מכיל 18', check (d) => d.includes("18"), points anchored to ~2-digit-substring frequency (≈4-7), unique lowercase English id, scoring.js still loads, no client files touched.

assertions.text: A new PLATE_PERKS entry exists with name 'חי' and desc 'מספר לוחית מכיל 18', both verbatim
The check returns true for a plate containing 18 (e.g. '12180000') and false for one without (e.g. '23456790')
The check is a substring test (d.includes("18") or equivalent), not a numeric coincidence
Points are in a sane range anchored to ~2-digit-substring rarity (roughly 4-7)
The id is unique, short, lowercase English
No files under public/ were modified

evals.name: mirror-halves-length-safe
evals.prompt: add a perk 'מראה' described 'חצי ראשון זהה לחצי השני' that applies when the first half of the plate equals the second half (like 1234-1234)
evals.expected_output: One new PLATE_PERKS entry: name 'מראה', desc 'חצי ראשון זהה לחצי השני'. check returns true for 8-digit plates whose first 4 digits equal the last 4 (e.g. '12341234'); crucially it must NOT throw on 7-digit plates. High-ish points (rare pattern). scoring.js still loads, no client files touched.

assertions.text: A new PLATE_PERKS entry exists with name 'מראה' and desc 'חצי ראשון זהה לחצי השני', both verbatim
The check returns true for '12341234' and false for '12341235'
The check does NOT throw on a 7-digit plate (e.g. '1234123') — verified by the estimator's throw warning being absent or a direct call
Points reflect that this is a rare pattern (clearly higher than a 2-digit-substring perk)
No files under public/ were modified

evals.name: only-8-or-9-helper-reuse
evals.prompt: add a perk 'אלופה' described 'כל הספרות הן 8 או 9' for plates where every digit is either 8 or 9
evals.expected_output: One new PLATE_PERKS entry: name 'אלופה', desc 'כל הספרות הן 8 או 9', check using isAllIn(d, "89") (reusing the existing helper). True for '8899'/'88998899', false for '8891'. Points high (very rare). scoring.js still loads, no client files touched.

assertions.text: A new PLATE_PERKS entry exists with name 'אלופה' and desc 'כל הספרות הן 8 או 9', both verbatim
The check returns true for '88998899' and false for '88918899'
The check reuses the existing isAllIn(d, "89") helper rather than re-implementing it
Points reflect a very rare pattern (high, clearly above mid-tier perks)
No files under public/ were modified
